experiment: Qwen3-ForcedAligner-0.6B vs current MMS-1B-all CTC pipeline, forced alignment for karaoke line sync
env: isolated uv venv (qwen-align-venv), torch 2.11.0+cu128, qwen-asr==0.0.6, RTX 5090, main venv untouched

model.license: Apache-2.0 (vs current MMS-1B-all CC-BY-NC-4.0 -- major licensing upgrade if it worked)
model.params: 601M (0.9B incl. audio encoder per HF card), ~1.84GB fp16/bf16
model.install: pip install qwen-asr (pulls torch/transformers deps); from qwen_asr import Qwen3ForcedAligner

perf.vram_after_several_calls_MiB: ~9200 (no explicit cache eviction observed between .align() calls in this package version)
perf.full_song_align_wall_s: 1.0-3.8s for a 241s / 989-1337 char song (RTF roughly 0.005-0.016, i.e. 60-250x realtime) -- inference itself is extremely fast when it doesn't fail

reliability_findings.test_song: 熱異常 (netsujou) -- original vocaloid b2NTglk9tvI + human cover hDhjRh-Gt4g, 134 lyric lines, both ~241s
reliability_findings.ground_truth: YouTube auto-captions SRT for hDhjRh-Gt4g cover (77 cues after collapsing repeated identical lines), same char-stream+SequenceMatcher methodology as srt_analysis/compare.py for apples-to-apples comparability

full_song_one_shot_call.method: single model.align() call: full audio file path + full concatenated lyric text (ja or ko-pronunciation)
full_song_one_shot_call.result: plausible timestamps for roughly the first 90-150s, then CATASTROPHIC FREEZE: remaining lines all pinned to one identical timestamp (e.g. 199.92s on the original audio) for the rest of the song -- 85/134 lines (63%) frozen on b2NTglk9tvI
full_song_one_shot_call.ko_pronunciation: same freeze pattern observed by direct inspection (could not score vs the ja-text SRT with the char-matching method -- language mismatch, not a model issue)
full_song_one_shot_call.reproduced_in_fp32_too: yes -- identical 199.92s freeze value with dtype=torch.float32, so this is NOT a bf16 precision artifact

control_song_stable_lyrics.song: ロキ (roki, Xg-qfsKN2_E.wav) -- non-repetitive lyrics, known near-perfect (median 0.04s) for the current MMS pipeline
control_song_stable_lyrics.result: SAME failure class even without repeated lyrics: e.g. one line assigned a 93s span (9.92-102.88), several consecutive lines frozen at 102.88s, and the LAST 5 lines got start=None/end=None (no timestamp produced at all)
control_song_stable_lyrics.conclusion: the freeze is not specific to 熱異常's repeated hooks -- it is a general instability of this model+package for ~4-minute full-song inputs

manual_chunking_attempt.method: cut a clean, correctly-matched 30s clip (same PCM_16/48kHz format as source) covering lines 0-7, align only that clip+text
manual_chunking_attempt.bf16_result: ALL items returned start=end=0.0 (complete degenerate failure) for every one of 1-8 lines tested
manual_chunking_attempt.fp32_result: FIXED -- non-degenerate, plausible timestamps close to ground truth (e.g. line0 0.0-2.24s vs SRT truth 0.27-3.42s)
manual_chunking_attempt.takeaway: bf16 causes a separate, additional short-clip failure mode not present in fp32; fp32 + manual VAD-style chunking looks like the only potentially viable integration path, but was only spot-checked on one 30s/8-line clip, not validated at full-song scale

current_pipeline_baseline_same_song_cove.source: srt_analysis/compare.py against the same hDhjRh-Gt4g SRT (post-processing/safeguards already applied, per team's recent commits)

verdict.license_and_packaging: clear win -- Apache-2.0 vs current CC-BY-NC-4.0, official ja+ko support, trivial pip install, tiny VRAM, very fast raw inference
verdict.accuracy_as_tested: NOT usable as a drop-in replacement today -- one-shot full-song alignment (the natural/documented usage pattern) fails catastrophically after roughly the first 90-150s of any ~4min song regardless of lyric repetition (median line-start error 47-76s vs current pipeline's 0.07-0.09s on the identical benchmark, i.e. 3+ orders of magnitude worse)
verdict.possible_path_forward: fp32 + external VAD/segment-based chunking (mirroring the Demucs+VAD infrastructure the current MMS pipeline already has) shows promise on a single spot-checked 30s clip, but building and validating that chunking layer is substantial additional engineering, not yet proven at scale, and qwen-asr is a very young package (v0.0.6)
verdict.recommendation: DO NOT integrate now. Revisit if/when qwen-asr matures (check for official long-audio/song guidance or chunking utilities) or if someone validates the fp32-chunked approach across a full song end-to-end.
